gate_question: Does this exact current protocol, with current live state, allow an unprivileged attacker to extract funds or rewards beyond entitlement?

candidate.title_or_claim: Missed-interval cap in _calculateIntervalsETHBuild allocates entire pool in one interval, enabling outsized incentive extraction via swapETHForTorusAndBuild
candidate.claimed_root_cause: The overflow-prevention cap at lines 1087-1089 sets _totalAmountForInterval = totalETHForBuild when missedIntervals >= 1440 (10 days at 10% daily allocation / 144 intervals/day). The incentive computation in swapETHForTorusAndBuild then pays 1.5% of the entire pool to msg.sender instead of 1.5% of a single normal interval allocation.
candidate.claimed_impact: Unprivileged caller extracts 1.5% of totalETHForBuild (~0.0016 ETH at current pool size, scaling with pool growth) in a single call, versus the intended ~0.007% of pool per normal interval call.

live_preconditions.name: No call to swapETHForTorusAndBuild for >= 1440 intervals (10 days at DAILY_ALLOCATION_ETH_BUILDING=1000)
live_preconditions.observed_value: lastETHBuildIntervalStartTimestamp=1784012400; capture timestamp ~1784023109; elapsed ~178 min (~17 intervals). Condition NOT yet met at capture time but reachable if function goes uncalled for ~10 days. BuyAndBuild event sample_count=0 in last 10000 blocks (~33 hours) indicates low call frequency.

source_code_basis.why_it_matters: Cap at line 1087-1089 sets _totalAmountForInterval = totalETHForBuild when amountPerInterval * (1 + missedIntervals) > totalETHForBuild. With DAILY_ALLOCATION_ETH_BUILDING=1000 and INTERVALS_PER_DAY=144, this triggers after missedIntervals >= 1440 (10 days). The cap was intended to prevent overflow but instead enables full-pool allocation in one interval.

Reads currInterval.amountAllocated (set by modifier to totalETHForBuild after cap), computes incentive = amountAllocated * 150 / 10000, and sends it to msg.sender via low-level call. No per-call maximum on incentive independent of the cap.

Modifier fires _intervalUpdateETHForBuilding() before function body, which calls _calculateIntervalsETHBuild and writes the capped amountAllocated into ethIntervalsBuild[lastETHBuildIntervalNumber]. The IntervalAlreadyUsed check at line 905 passes because the freshly created interval has amountBurned=0.
INCENTIVE_FEE_BUILD=150, BPS_DENOM=10_000, INTERVALS_PER_DAY=144, INTERVAL_TIME=10 minutes. These are immutable constants confirming the arithmetic.

attacker_inputs: _deadline: any valid future uint32 timestamp (e.g. block.timestamp + 300)
call_sequence: 1. Wait until block.timestamp - lastETHBuildIntervalStartTimestamp > 1440 * 600 = 864000 seconds (~10 days) with no intervening calls to swapETHForTorusAndBuild

3. buildETHIntervalUpdate modifier fires _intervalUpdateETHForBuilding() -> _calculateIntervalsETHBuild(timeElapsed) returns missedIntervals >= 1440, _totalAmountForInterval = totalETHForBuild (capped)
6. incentive = totalETHForBuild * 150 / 10000 is computed and sent to msg.sender
state_after: totalETHForBuild reduced by totalETHForBuild (entire pool consumed in one interval)

extraction_analysis.asset_or_reward: Native ETH from totalETHForBuild pool held by TorusBuyAndProcess contract
extraction_analysis.attacker_gain: 1.5% of totalETHForBuild = ~1,599,520,501,288,770 wei (~0.0016 ETH at current pool size). Scales proportionally as pool grows via distributeETHForBuilding.
extraction_analysis.victim_or_protocol_loss: Protocol loses 1.5% of the entire ETH build pool in a single call instead of 1.5% of a single normal interval allocation (~0.007% of pool). Excess extraction = (1.5% * totalETHForBuild) - (1.5% * normalIntervalAllocation) = approximately 1440x the intended per-call incentive.
extraction_analysis.why_gain_exceeds_entitlement: The intended incentive per call is 1.5% of one interval's allocation = 1.5% * (totalETHForBuild * 10% / 144) = ~0.001% of pool. The cap causes the entire pool to be treated as one interval's allocation, so the caller receives 1.5% of the entire pool. This is ~1440x the intended incentive and is not documented as expected behavior anywhere in NatSpec or README.

setup: Record attacker ETH balance before call
expected_assertions: assertGt(attacker.balance - balBefore, normalIntervalIncentive * 100) // attacker got >> 100x normal incentive
local_proof_required.reject_if_assertion_fails: If balAfter - balBefore equals only a single-interval normal incentive (not the capped full-pool incentive), the vulnerability does not exist as described.
